ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (noodle-based dish) used as the closest defensible broader category; no specific code exists for spicy dumpling stew noodles in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of spicy dumpling stew noodles is approximately 400 g, comprising noodles, several chive dumplings, hotdog slices, corn kernels, crabstick pieces, and spicy stew broth.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Dumpling Stew Noodles — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Dumpling Stew Noodles (cr_id G00010641). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB source; saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous mixed noodle dish data; dietary fibre is estimated at 1.5 g/100g based on ingredient composition (noodles, vegetables, dumpling wrappers) using USDA SR Legacy reference values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; noodle dish analogous estimate based on wheat noodle soup GI ~52
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spicy Dumpling Stew Noodles. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the closest analogous food: wheat-based noodle soup with mixed protein components, as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Glycemic load calculated as: GI (52) × carbohydrate per serving (8.03 g/100g × 400 g serving = 32.1 g) / 100 = 16.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 46 mg/100g — exceptionally low for a spicy noodle dish
hypertension.guidance: The reported sodium of 46 mg/100g is well below the 200 mg/100g threshold and is suitable for hypertensive patients; however, this value likely reflects solid components only — the spicy broth (soy sauce, chilli paste) can contribute 400–800 mg sodium/100g if consumed, so patients should avoid drinking the broth and request reduced soy sauce and chilli paste in preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.0 g/100g), high protein (8.7 g/100g), low GI (~52), negligible sugar (0.2 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content, high protein-to-carbohydrate ratio, and estimated low GI of approximately 52 make this dish suitable for type 2 diabetic patients in moderate portions; patients should practise portion control given the refined carbohydrate in dumpling wrappers and request additional vegetables to increase dietary fibre and further blunt post-meal glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol 28 mg/100g; processed ingredients (hotdog, crabstick); estimated saturated fat ~1.2 g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish contains processed meat (hotdog sausages) and imitation crabstick which contribute saturated fat, cholesterol, and potentially hidden sodium from the broth; cardiovascular patients should limit portion size, avoid drinking the broth, and request substitution of hotdog with lean protein such as tofu or chicken to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Likely pork or mixed-meat dumpling filling rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6); chilli as nightshade ingredient
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is not recommended for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to the likely red-meat dumpling filling which is rich in arachidonic acid and amplifies inflammatory pathways; patients should request vegetable or tofu dumplings and reduced chilli to make the dish RA-suitable, while retaining beneficial anti-inflammatory components such as bok choy and mushrooms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Dumpling Stew Noodles is a Singaporean noodle dish consisting of wheat-based noodles served in a spicy stew broth, accompanied by chive-filled dumplings (jiaozi), sliced hotdog sausages, corn kernels, and crabstick pieces. The dish is characterised by a bold, spicy, and savoury flavour profile derived from chilli paste and soy sauce in the broth, balanced by the mild sweetness of corn and the umami of crabstick. The noodles are soft and absorb the spicy broth, while the dumplings provide a chewy, filled bite. Per 100 g, the dish provides 101 kcal, 8.7 g protein, 3.8 g fat, and 8.0 g carbohydrate, with negligible sugar (0.2 g) and a notably low reported sodium of 46 mg — though this likely reflects solid components only, as the broth would substantially increase sodium intake if consumed. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 52 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management in moderate portions. The high protein-to-carbohydrate ratio supports satiety and moderates post-meal glucose response. Clinically, the dish is suitable for hypertensive and type 2 diabetic patients when broth consumption is minimised and portion size is controlled; it is not recommended for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to the likely red-meat dumpling filling. The dish is commonly consumed as lunch or dinner in Singapore and is associated with hawker centre and food court settings.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010641) with HPB-assigned confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous noodle dish data; dietary fibre estimated from ingredient composition using USDA SR Legacy. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous wheat noodle soup entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010641, and no sufficiently close equivalent entry was found in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous mixed noodle dish with processed meat components (USDA SR Legacy). Dietary fibre (1.5 g/100g) is estimated from ingredient composition (wheat noodles, dumpling wrappers, corn, chive) using USDA SR Legacy reference values. The reported sodium value of 46 mg/100g likely reflects solid components only; actual sodium intake including broth consumption would be substantially higher (estimated 400–800 mg/100g for the full dish with broth). Halal status is uncertain due to the presence of hotdog sausages whose meat source is unspecified; is_halal set to false pending verification. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on wheat noodle soup with mixed protein from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found.
